content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


June 10, 1986


Michael Metzler, President
Greater Santa Ana Chamber of Commerce
Santa Ana Community Political Action
Committee
600 West Santa Ana Blvd.
P.O. Box 205
Santa Ana, CA 92702
                                                Re: Your Request for Advice
                                                    FPPC No. A-86-160

Dear Mr. Metzler:

     This is in response to your letter, dated May 8, 1986,
requesting written confirmation of the telephone advice I provided
to you. The advice I provided as presented in your attached
letter is correct.

     As we discussed on the telephone, Santa Ana Community
Political Action Committee (SACPAC), a general purpose committee,
will not be receiving earmarked contributions in connection with
the Measure C effort.

     SACPAC intends to pay some of the bills already incurred by
the Santa Ana Good Government Committee, also a general purpose
committee. The bills are a result of opposition to Measure C
appearing on the Santa Ana June 3, 1986 ballot. These payments by
SACPAC constitute contributions to the Santa Ana Good Government
Committee. SACPAC must report these expenditures on Schedule E,
indicating in the "Description of Payment" column that the
payments are being made on behalf of the Santa Ana Good Government
Committee.

     If either committee has made independent expenditures to
oppose Measure C, a Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report,
Form 465, must be filed in addition to the Form 420. An
independent expenditure is an expenditure made in connection with
a communication which expressly advocates the election or defeat
of a clearly identified candidate or the qualification, passage or
defeat of a clearly identified measure which is not made to or at
the behest of the affected candidate or committee. Made at the


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Michael Metzler
June 10, 1986
Page Two


behest means made under the control or at the direction of, in
cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with, or at the
request or suggestion of a candidate or a committee primarily
formed to support or oppose a candidate or measure.

     If you have any questions concerning the Form 465, or any
other questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,
                                        [signature]
                                        Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Political Reform Consultant

MAK:kt
Attachment

Greater
Santa Ana
Chamber of
Commerce

600 West Santa Ana Boulevard
P.O. Box 205
Santa Ana, California 92702
(714) 541-5353

MAY 12 5 31 AM 80

May 8, 1986


Ms. Mary Ann Kvasager
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Kvasager:

Please accept this letter as a follow-up to our telephone conversation.

The Greater Santa Ana Chamber of Commerce has recently formed a general
purpose PAC, called Santa Ana Community Political Action Committee
(SACPAC). SACPAC is assisting another general purpose PAC, called
the Santa Ana Good Government Committee (Good Government Committee)
in opposing a local ballot measure C, for which the election will
be held on June 3.

Contributions for SACPAC are being accepted for the general purposes
of the PAC which includes opposing Measure C. SACPAC is then making
contributions to the Good Government Committee regarding Measure C.

In our telephone conversation it was determined by you that the fol-
lowing options could be exercised by SACPAC:

     1)  SACPAC could receive contributions regarding Measure C
         and then make contributions from SACPAC to the Good
         Government Committee; or,

     2)  SACPAC could receive contributions regarding Measure C,
         pay expenses of Measure C as incurred by the Good Govern-
         ment Committee, and then list that contribution as an
         in-kind contribution to the Good Government Committee.

Would you please corroborate this by written correspondence?

An immediate response is greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Michael Metzler
President

MM:bcc

/

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


May 13, 1986


Michael Metzler
Greater Santa Ana
Chamber of Commerce
P.O. Box 205
Santa Ana, Ca 92702

Re: 86-160

Dear Mr. Metzler:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been received on May 12, 1986 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

sections.question_synthetic: How should Santa Ana Community Political Action Committee (SACPAC), a general purpose committee, handle contributions it receives regarding Measure C when making payments to support the Santa Ana Good Government Committee's opposition to that measure?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: SACPAC may receive contributions for general purposes including Measure C opposition and make contributions to the Good Government Committee; these payments must be reported on Schedule E as contributions to that committee. If independent expenditures were made to oppose Measure C, Form 465 must be filed in addition to Form 420.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (May 8, 1986) and the FPPC response letter (June 10, 1986). The FPPC response confirms previously given telephone advice. The document also includes a receipt acknowledgment letter (May 13, 1986). Extraction based on the substantive FPPC advice letter dated June 10, 1986.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: SACPAC intends to receive contributions regarding Measure C and make contributions to the Good Government Committee. Can SACPAC receive contributions regarding Measure C and then make contributions from SACPAC to the Good Government Committee, or should SACPAC receive contributions regarding Measure C, pay expenses of Measure C as incurred by the Good Government Committee, and then list that contribution as an in-kind contribution to the Good Government Committee?

CONCLUSION: The advice I provided as presented in your attached letter is correct. As we discussed on the telephone, Santa Ana Community Political Action Committee (SACPAC), a general purpose committee, will not be receiving earmarked contributions in connection with the Measure C effort. SACPAC intends to pay some of the bills already incurred by the Santa Ana Good Government Committee, also a general purpose committee. The bills are a result of opposition to Measure C appearing on the Santa Ana June 3, 1986 ballot. These payments by SACPAC constitute contributions to the Santa Ana Good Government Committee. SACPAC must report these expenditures on Schedule E, indicating in the "Description of Payment" column that the payments are being made on behalf of the Santa Ana Good Government Committee. If either committee has made independent expenditures to oppose Measure C, a Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report, Form 465, must be filed in addition to the Form 420.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms prior telephone advice to SACPAC regarding how to handle contributions received for opposing Measure C and payments made to the Santa Ana Good Government Committee, requiring Schedule E reporting and potential Form 465 filing for independent expenditures.
